name: DeepSeek V3
brainrotOneliner: 671B parameters but only 37B doing actual werk at any time. efficiency arc unlocked.

brainrotStats.activeParams: main character active path (37B actually firing)

keyInnovations: Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) compresses KV cache into latent vectors — massive memory savings
DeepSeekMoE uses 256 fine-grained experts with 1 shared + 8 routed active per token
Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) auxiliary objective improves training efficiency
Trained for only $5.6M — the cost efficiency claim that shook the industry
brainrotSummary: DeepSeek looked at dense transformers and said 'why activate everything when you can be selective'. MoE routing means only a fraction of parameters fire per token — maximum efficiency, minimum NPC energy. the $5.6M training cost claim sent the entire AI industry into a crisis of confidence. this is the model that made everyone question whether the expensive approach was ever necessary.

comparisons.gpt2: GPT-2 is the NPC ancestor. DeepSeek V3 is 5 years and a different country's AI ambitions later.
comparisons.gpt3: GPT-3 proved scale works. DeepSeek V3 proved you can be smarter about it.
comparisons.llama2: LLaMA 2 opened the era. DeepSeek V3 took it to a different level with MoE.
comparisons.gpt4: DeepSeek V3 is the first open model to genuinely threaten GPT-4's status. the west is nervous.
comparisons.claude3: DeepSeek efficiency vs Anthropic safety. different threat models, different architectures.
comparisons.llama3-instruct: LLaMA 3 dense vs DeepSeek sparse. meta's scale vs deepseek's efficiency. both winning.
comparisons.mixtral-8x7b: both MoE, different era. Mixtral proved the concept. DeepSeek V3 perfected it.
comparisons.grok-1: both went big with MoE. DeepSeek was more efficient about it. the receipts are public.
